# ccc — call coding CLIs

> One command to run any coding CLI. A unified interface implemented in 20 programming languages.

ccc is a command-line tool that wraps terminal-first coding agents (OpenCode, Claude Code, Codex, Kimi, Crush) behind a single consistent interface. Each of the 20 language implementations bundles an identical `ccc` CLI binary and a reusable library with `CommandSpec`, `CompletedRun`, and `Runner` types.

## Usage

ccc [runner] [+thinking] [:provider:model] [@name] "<Prompt>"

Slots are positional and parsed in order before the prompt:
- runner: Select which coding CLI (default: oc). Options: opencode (oc), claude (cc), kimi (k), codex (c/cx), roocode (rc), crush (cr)
- +thinking: Set thinking level from +0 (off) to +4 (max)
  Claude keeps the same `ccc` scale: `+0` becomes `--thinking disabled`, and `+1..+4` become `--thinking enabled` with matching `--effort`.
  Kimi keeps the same `ccc` scale: `+0` becomes `--no-thinking`, and `+1..+4` become `--thinking`.
- :provider:model: Override provider and model
- @name: Use a named preset from ~/.config/ccc/config.toml, or treat it as an agent when no preset exists

Examples:
- ccc "Fix the failing tests"
- ccc cc +2 :anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-20250514 "Add tests"
- ccc k +4 "Debug the parser"

## Languages

Implemented in: Python, Rust, TypeScript, C, Go, Ruby, Perl, C++, Zig, D, F#, Haskell, Nim, Crystal, PHP, PureScript, Elixir, OCaml, VBScript, x86-64 ASM.

All implementations share: build_prompt_spec, CommandSpec, CompletedRun, Runner.run, ccc CLI, prompt trimming, empty prompt rejection, usage on bad args, CCC_REAL_OPENCODE env var, exit code forwarding, and startup failure reporting.

18 of 20 languages also implement: v2 parser/config (runner selector, thinking levels, provider/model override, preset and agent resolution, config loading) and v3 JSON output parsing (OpenCode, Claude Code, Kimi schemas).

## Docs

- [CLI Reference](https://xertrov.github.io/call-coding-clis/): Full usage, slots, examples, feature matrix, build and test instructions

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/xertrov/call-coding-clis): Source code, all 20 language implementations
